headline: Singapore charged three over Nvidia chips routed via Malaysia. Johor kept building.
context: On 27 Feb 2025, Singapore charged three men with fraud tied to servers carrying Nvidia AI chips that moved Singapore to Malaysia. Johor has approved more data-centre capacity since. The US export-control framework over those chips is still being rewritten.

cards.big: On 27 February 2025, Singapore charged three men over the sale of servers carrying Nvidia AI chips. The route ran Singapore to Malaysia.
cards.sub: US prosecutors are investigating whether chips that landed in Malaysia were diverted to a Chinese AI firm. Johor approved more capacity that quarter.

cards.big: Washington classifies Malaysia as a 'Tier 2' diversion-risk country for AI chips, even after the rule was rescinded.
cards.sub: BIS issued the AI Diffusion Rule on 13 Jan 2025 placing Malaysia in Tier 2. Lutnick rescinded it 13 May 2025 and replaced it with case-by-case licensing plus a diversion warning.

cards.big: Johor has 80+ approved data-centre projects and over 5 GW of announced capacity, roughly a quarter of Peninsular Malaysia's peak power demand.
cards.sub: Microsoft (USD 2.2B), Google (USD 2B), AWS, ByteDance and YTL are anchor tenants. The thesis assumes uninterrupted access to US-controlled AI chips.

cards.big: MITI said it would investigate any Malaysian company named in a US diversion probe. No list has been made public.
cards.sub: Trade Minister Tengku Zafrul gave that commitment in March 2025. The Strategic Trade Act 2010 carries fines to RM1M and 5 years' jail for unlicensed strategic-item export.

cards.big: Johor's data-centre boom and Singapore's chip case tell one story: Washington decides which chips Malaysia gets to run.
Like building the largest port on a known smuggling route, then asking the customs office across the border to keep clearing it.
cards.sub: Johor's data-centre build-out runs on US-controlled chips. Three men were charged across the strait for moving them.

cards.big: Singapore charged three men in February. The chips moved through Malaysia. Johor approved more capacity. Washington decides what happens next.
